messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agent Picker is not pre-selected when creating a new Agent Host session with specific Agent in Agent Window
ISSUE BODY: Blocked on https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/317848

**Steps to repro**
* Start a new Chat Session in Agents Window
* Select `vscode` fodler
* Select `Copilot CLI Local Agent Host`
* Select a custom agent from the agent picker
* Enter the prompt `what is the time`
* Hit the send button
* Then wait for new session to start
* You'll see that the new chat session doesn't have the agent selected in the Agent Picker

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent host system-prompt customization  
This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host**
(ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's
`extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent Host  
The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory.  
If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:///<path/to/agent-host-protocol>/plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Enabling Claude Sessions  
Claude sessions are **enabled by default**. If you don't see Claude in the session target picker, verify the setting is on:  
1. Open VS Code Settings (`Cmd+,` / `Ctrl+,`)
2. Search for `claudeAgent.enabled`
3. Ensure **"Enable Claude Agent sessions in VS Code"** is checked  
To disable Claude sessions, set this in your `settings.json`:  
```json
{
"github.copilot.chat.claudeAgent.enabled": false
}
```

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## End to End Testing  
You can run `node ./scripts/code-agent-host.js` to start an agent host. If you pass `--enable-mock-agent`, then the `ScriptedMockAgent` will be used.  
By default this will listen on `ws://127.0.0.1:8081`. You can then use the `ahp-websocket` client, when available, to connect to and communicate with it.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\ai-customization.instructions.md =====  
--- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/**'
---

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Iterate
1. Draft the agent file and save it.
2. Identify the most ambiguous or weak parts and ask about those.
3. Once finalized, summarize what the agent does, suggest example prompts to try it, and propose related customizations to create next.  
Remember to follow the `agent-customization` guidelines to create highly effective agents.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\extensions\copilot\assets\prompts\skills\create-hook\SKILL.md =====  
--- name: create-hook description: 'Create a hook (.json) to enforce policy or automate agent lifecycle events.' argument-hint: What should be enforced or automated?
disable-model-invocation: true
---
Related skill: `agent-customization`. Load and follow **hooks.md** for template and principles.  
Guide the user to create a hook in `.github/hooks/`.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Agent Picker should pre-select the connected agent when starting a new Agent Host session",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "ui-sync", "session-management"],
  "jira_linked_items": [
    "https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/317848"
  ],
  "user_story": "As a Copilot user, I want the Agent Picker to automatically display and pre-select the agent that was connected when starting a new session in the Agents Window, so that I can continue my conversation without manually selecting the same agent again.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a user has selected an agent from the picker before sending a message",
    "When they wait for a new Agent Host session to start after hitting send",
    "Then the Agent Picker in the newly created chat session should show and pre-select that same agent"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Review Agent Host Protocol specification for session creation flow",
      "Identify where agent selection state is stored during initial connection",
      "Add mechanism to persist agent identity when new WebSocket session is established",
      "Ensure agent context is passed from Agent Host process back to VS Code UI"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update Agent Picker component to receive and display connected agent information",
      "Implement pre-selection logic in chat session initialization flow",
      "Add visual feedback when agent selection is automatically applied"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test new session creation with different agents selected from picker",
      "Verify agent name correctly appears in Agent Picker for subsequent messages",
      "Confirm behavior works across multiple consecutive sessions",
      "Validate edge cases: agent unselected, agent changed mid-session"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by backend and frontend team members",
    "All acceptance criteria tests passed in QA environment",
    "Agent selection persists correctly across at least 3 consecutive sessions",
    "No regressions introduced to existing Agent Host functionality"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the expected behavior if the user changes agents mid-session? Should it update automatically?",
    "Is there a specific protocol message that should carry agent selection state from Agent Host to VS Code?",
    "Should this work for all agent types (Copilot CLI, Claude, custom agents) or just Copilot CLI Local Agent Host?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The Agent Host Protocol specification exists and defines session creation flow",
    "Agent selection state is currently lost during new WebSocket connection establishment",
    "This is primarily a UI synchronization issue rather than a fundamental protocol limitation"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent host system-prompt customization section",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Host communication protocol documentation",
    "Issue body with reproduction steps"
  ]
}
```
